name: docx
tagline: Guides Claude to build, edit, and analyze Word .docx files via docx-js and XML unpack/edit/pack scripts, including tracked changes.
verdict: Solid, safe skill: clear use/don't-use triggers, deterministic docx-js and XML guidance, plus validation/auto-repair steps. No secret access, network exfiltration, or destructive ops. Minor issues: file runs 592 lines (over the ~500 budget), `npm install -g docx` is unpinned (supply-chain drift risk), and repeated unpack/edit/pack runs have no guidance again

highlights.summary: Line 59: `npm install -g docx` installs the third-party npm package globally with no version pin, so behavior can silently drift on new releases (supply-chain risk, though the package itself is a well

No guidance on ID collisions (w:id for w:ins/w:del, comment ids) or repeatability if unpack→edit→pack is run multiple times against the same document; re-running edits could produce duplicate revision
File is 592 lines, moderately over the ~500-line budget, though content density (docx-js API + XML schema reference) partly justifies the length.
